question_text: Explain why ice floats on water even though both are made of the same substance (H₂O).
correct_answer: Ice floats because it has a lower density than water. When water freezes, its particles arrange in a way that takes up more space, making ice less dense than liquid water.
explanation: This is an unusual property of water: the solid form (ice) is less dense than the liquid form. This happens because of how water molecules arrange when frozen, creating more space between them. Most other substances have denser solids than liquids, which is why ice floating is special and important for aquatic life.
